title: ggen Developer Experience (DX) Definition of Done
description: Specification of seven DX dimensions that define what makes ggen development frictionless and productive. All dimensions must be satisfied for DX to be complete.

dimensions.name: Command Ergonomics
dimensions.description: What `just` commands must exist? All critical commands must be available as single-word recipes with predictable behavior and clear semantics.
dimensions.whyItMatters: Developers spend 30% of time running build/test commands. Each missing recipe or unclear command name creates context-switching overhead and mental load. Ergonomic commands reduce friction from dev loop startup to test failures to performance debugging.
successCriteria: All critical workflows have a corresponding `just <task>` recipe
Commands are discoverable via `just --list`
All recipes include descriptive comments explaining purpose
Recipes timeout gracefully (no infinite hangs)
Exit codes are meaningful (0 = success, non-zero = failure)
All recipes are idempotent (safe to run multiple times)
First-time execution provides helpful guidance if prerequisites missing

implemented: pre-commit — fmt-check → check → lint → test-lib (sequential, fail-fast)
gaps: test-unit alias (faster than test for dev loop)
watch/continuous test runner (for hot-reload iteration)
perf profiling recipe (flamegraph, criterion)
speckit-validate — RDF specification validation
speckit-render — Generate markdown from TTL specs
otel-verify — Run tests with OTEL tracing enabled
ci — Full CI validation (check + test + lint + slo-check + audit)
docs-serve — Local docs browsing
release — Semantic versioning bump + changelog + tag

core-loop.test-unit: Unit/lib tests only (<10s hot cache) — fastest feedback loop
core-loop.pre-commit: fmt-check → check → lint → test-lib (<2min) — gate before commit

validation.test: Full test suite (<30s hot, <120s first) — integration + unit
validation.test-doc: Doctest validation — ensure all /// Examples compile/run
validation.test-marketplace: Pack layer validation — fast gate without network
validation.slo-check: Performance SLO validation — ensure latency targets met
validation.ci: Full pipeline (check + lint + test + slo-check + audit)

rdf-pipeline.sync: Full μ₁-μ₅ sync with cryptographic receipt

developer-experience.watch: Continuous test runner (auto-run on file change)
developer-experience.otel-verify: Run tests with RUST_LOG=trace for OTEL verification

examples.output: ✅ llm.complete span found with llm.model, llm.total_tokens attributes

dimensions.name: Error Messages
dimensions.description: What makes error messages helpful? Clear, actionable, traceable errors that guide developers to root cause and recovery path.
dimensions.whyItMatters: Developers spend 40% of time debugging. A cryptic error message costs 15-30 minutes of investigation. A clear, actionable error costs 2 minutes. Error message quality directly impacts dev speed and frustration.
successCriteria: All errors use `thiserror` crate for structured error types
Error messages follow pattern: [ERROR CODE] What happened — Why it happened — How to fix it
Error codes are canonical (e.g., GGEN-TPL-001, E0011) and documented
Errors include context (file path, line number, failing value)
Errors suggest recovery actions when available
Stack traces are meaningful (not truncated or swallowed)
Validation errors include which constraint violated and why
Compiler errors preserved (not wrapped in way that hides detail)
CLI errors include `--help` suggestion when applicable
JSON diagnostics emitted for tooling (LSP, IDE integration)

compilationErrors.requirement: Preserve full rustc output; never suppress

examples.GGEN-TPL-001: Template consumes {{ var }} that SELECT does not produce
examples.E0011: CONSTRUCT lacks ORDER BY (Strict Mode: ERROR)

runtimeErrors.template:
[ERROR] ggen sync failed
  Cause: pack 'acme/base' not found in .ggen/packs.lock
  Fix: ggen packs add acme/base && ggen sync --locked

andonSignals.action: STOP_THE_LINE — read error, fix cause, re-run check

implemented: ValidationError enum with thiserror (file not found, permission denied, path traversal, etc.)
Diagnostic codes (GGEN-*) defined in validation layer
ggen-lsp emits LSP Diagnostic messages with codes
Andon signals defined for compiler errors, test failures, warnings
gaps: Not all error types document recovery actions
Error messages don't always include file:line context
CLI help for each command not cross-linked in errors
No centralized error catalogue (all codes documented in one place)
Validation errors sometimes vague about constraint violated

contents: All GGEN-* codes with description, example, recovery path
Common rustc error codes (E0308, etc.) with ggen context
How to read Andon signals and what to do

examples.goodError:
[ERROR] GGEN-RULE-001: Pack 'acme/base' not found
  Expected: .ggen/packs/acme/base/pack.toml
  Fix: ggen packs add acme/base
  Or: Check .ggen/packs.lock — pack may have been deleted

[ERROR] GGEN-TPL-001: Template uses {{ package_version }} but SELECT binds only {{ package_id }}
  Query: crates/ggen-core/queries/packages.rq (line 12)
  Template: templates/package.tera (line 5)
  Fix: Either (a) add ?package_version to SELECT, or (b) remove {{ package_version }} from template

[ERROR] test_foo::test_bar FAILED
  Expected: 100 items
  Got: 99 items
  Likely cause: Off-by-one in loop (line 45)
  Fix: Check .specify/test_foo/feature.ttl — may need to adjust expected cardinality

dimensions.name: Workflow Efficiency
dimensions.description: What keyboard shortcuts, aliases, and automation must work? Reduce typing, reduce context-switching, reduce manual steps.
dimensions.whyItMatters: Developers type ~500 commands per day. A 10-character alias saves 50 keystrokes = 30 seconds per session. Over a year, that's 40+ hours. Workflow efficiency is compounding: every saved keystroke multiplies across the entire team.
successCriteria: Shell aliases exist for common commands (jt=just test-unit, etc.)
`.just.local` file supports per-developer aliases without cluttering repo
.claude/hooks auto-run on key events (SessionStart, PreToolUse, Stop)
Git hooks run pre-commit and pre-push gates automatically
RUST_LOG environment variables pre-configured for common scenarios
Cargo aliases configured (e.g., `cargo t` for test, `cargo c` for check)
IDE keybindings configured for run, debug, test
File watchers auto-trigger test suite on save
Tab completion for `just` commands in shell
Quick access to docs (Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+D opens local docs)

implemented: .claude/hooks for SessionStart, UserPromptSubmit, Stop, PreToolUse, PostToolUse
gaps: No shell alias definitions (user must create .justrc or .zshrc)
No cargo aliases in config (user must add [alias] to .cargo/config.toml)
No git hooks for pre-commit/pre-push gates (would require .git/hooks/)
No file watcher recipe (just watch not implemented)
No IDE keybindings configured (.vscode/keybindings.json not committed)
No tab completion for `just` (would require shell-specific setup)
No quick-docs keybinding documented

shellAliases.content:
# Add to your shell profile
alias jt='just test-unit'
alias ju='just test'
alias jc='just check'
alias jl='just lint'
alias jp='just pre-commit'
alias js='just sync'
alias jsd='just sync-dry'
alias jd='just doc'

cargoAliases.content:
[alias]
c = "check"
t = "test"
l = "lint"
f = "fmt"
b = "build"
tr = "tree"

examples.before: cargo test --lib --workspace && cargo check --workspace && cargo clippy --all-targets
cargo fmt --all && cargo check --workspace && cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings && git add . && git commit -m '...'

dimensions.name: IDE Integration
dimensions.description: What LSP, debugging, and editor support must exist? Semantic navigation, real-time diagnostics, debugging without printf.
dimensions.whyItMatters: IDE integration bridges code and understanding. Without LSP, developers grep blindly. Without diagnostics, they wait for CI. Without debugging, they print!() and guess. Full IDE support cuts debugging time by 70%.
successCriteria: LSP (rust-analyzer) fully enabled and configured
Real-time diagnostics show errors as-you-type
Go-to-definition works across crate boundaries (including re-exports)
Find-references finds all usages in workspace
Hover shows type signatures, trait bounds, visibility
Inline type hints for variables and return types
Code completion suggests from workspace symbols
Quick-fix suggestions for common errors (add import, derive, etc.)
Debugger configured (lldb for Mac, gdb for Linux)
Breakpoints work in tests and binaries
REPL/watch expressions during debugging
Ggen-lsp diagnostics (GGEN-* codes) appear in editor
RDF validation diagnostics appear in-editor for .ttl files
Markdown preview for generated .md files

implemented: ggen-lsp-mcp exposes repair routes as MCP tool
gaps: No .vscode/settings.json committed (users must set up manually)
No .vscode/keybindings.json for debugging shortcuts
No .vscode/launch.json for debug configurations
No .lldbinit or .gdbinit for debugger setup
No VSCode extension for ggen-specific diagnostics
No markdown preview template for generated .md files
No Vim/Neovim configuration documented

mac.config:
~/.lldbinit contains:
settings set target.process.follow-fork-mode child
settings set target.process.stop-on-exec true

linux.config:
~/.gdbinit contains:
handle SIGPIPE nostop noprint pass

examples.before: Search for where PackRegistry is defined across 15 crates
examples.after: Ctrl+Click on PackRegistry → jumps to definition in ggen-marketplace

examples.before: grep -r 'fn sync' crates/*/src/ | manually check each
examples.after: Cmd+Shift+H on 'sync' → lists all 47 call sites across workspace

examples.before: Hover over variable to see type, then reason about generics
examples.after: Type hints inline: let graph: OxigraphWrapper<T> = ...

examples.before: Run ggen validate, read terminal output, find file/line
examples.after: GGEN-TPL-001 warning appears red-squiggly in editor at template file:line

examples.before: println!("value={:?}", x); cargo test; search logs
examples.after: Set breakpoint, Cmd+Shift+D, step through, watch x in REPL

dimensions.name: Documentation Surface
dimensions.description: What quick references must be available in-editor? Instant answers without context-switching to browser.
dimensions.whyItMatters: Context-switching costs 15 minutes per switch (time to re-focus, time to find answer, time to navigate back). Embedding docs in the editor cuts this to 30 seconds. Developers look up: error meanings (20%), command syntax (30%), API signatures (40%), architectural patterns (10%).
successCriteria: Quick-access help available via `just --help` or `ggen --help`
`ggen <cmd> --help` shows command signature + examples + recovery paths
Error codes link to docs (e.g., 'Run: ggen docs GGEN-TPL-001')
Quick reference card available in .claude/
LSP hover shows docstring for every public symbol
Doctest /// Examples for all public APIs
Architecture reference in .claude/rules/architecture.md (live inventory)
CLAUDE.md embedded with rules and principles (checked into repo)
Diagnostic codes documented in centralized catalogue
Keyboard shortcuts documented in .claude/ with IDE integration guide

implemented: CLAUDE.md (10,000+ lines) documents full architecture, rules, commands
.claude/settings.json with env vars and hook configuration
justfile with comments on each recipe
Cargo.toml with project description and keywords
README.md with quickstart and project overview
docs/ directory with architecture and design docs
gaps: No quick-reference cheatsheet (2-page one-pager) for common commands
No in-editor help command (would require CLI integration)
Error codes not centralized (scattered across validation layer)
LSP hover docstrings incomplete (many functions lack /// comments)
No quick-access keyboard shortcut guide
No 'first 5 minutes' walkthrough for new developers
No troubleshooting guide (what to do when things break)

sections: Command Quick Reference (just check, just test, etc.)

items: Common errors and how to fix

examples.required: cat .claude/FIRST_TIME_HERE.md leads through setup in 5 min
examples.proof: Developer runs `just test-unit` and sees 347 tests pass

examples.required: Hover over error code → shows definition + recovery path
examples.proof: Developer knows exactly what to fix without googling

examples.scenario: Wants to use `just sync` but doesn't know flags
examples.required: just sync --help shows purpose, flags, examples, common errors
examples.proof: Developer runs `just sync --dry-run` and sees preview without fear
Developer finds answer in < 1 minute without context-switching

dimensions.name: Hot Reload & Iteration
dimensions.description: What fast-feedback loops must exist? cargo watch, test-unit, incremental builds, instant error feedback.
dimensions.whyItMatters: Developer psychology: feedback loop > 5 seconds breaks flow state. < 2 seconds maintains focus. Each code change → wait for feedback takes 1-3 seconds (focus) + 10-60 seconds (compile/test). Faster loops = more iterations = better code = less frustration.
successCriteria: test-unit runs in < 10 seconds (hot cache)
check runs in < 5 seconds
Incremental builds < 2 seconds for single-file changes
`just watch` recipe runs tests on file change (cargo watch integration)
LSP diagnostics appear as-you-type (< 500ms latency)
Compiler errors shown instantly (not after full compile)
First build < 15 seconds (SLO)
Cargo incremental build cache always enabled
sccache configured for distributed caching (optional but recommended)
REPL/WASM hot reload for non-CLI features (future)
Test output streaming (not buffered)
Flamegraph/perf profiling integration

implemented: test-unit (<30s hot cache, <120s first) with graceful timeout escalation
SCCACHE_DIR configured (read-only filesystem disables actual caching)
incremental compilation enabled by default in Cargo
gaps: just watch recipe not implemented (cargo watch not invoked)
No profiling recipe (flamegraph, criterion setup)
No REPL integration documented
No live-reload for documentation changes
LSP cold-start > 3 seconds (rust-analyzer initial analysis)
No incremental test runner (runs full suite, not changed tests only)

watch.purpose: Run tests + lint automatically on file change

otelVerify.purpose: Run tests with OTEL tracing enabled

examples.feedback: Run `jc && jl && jt` → all gates pass (< 2 min)

examples.setup: Split terminal: `just watch` in one pane, edit code in editor
examples.feedback: On every save, tests re-run instantly (< 2s)

dimensions.name: Sensible Defaults
dimensions.description: What sensible defaults reduce cognitive load? Env vars pre-set, config pre-populated, conventions established.
dimensions.whyItMatters: Cognitive load = decision fatigue. Developers should not decide: What RUST_LOG to use? Which test profile? How to handle errors? Sensible defaults reduce 100 decisions to 10. Conventions eliminate 'how should I structure this?'
successCriteria: RUST_LOG set to sensible default (info for prod, debug for tests)
RNG_SEED=42 for deterministic testing
RUST_BACKTRACE=1 for better error traces
CARGO_TERM_COLOR=always for readable output
Test isolation default (TempDir, tmpfs)
Error handling pattern established (Result<T,E>, not panics)
Naming conventions documented (snake_case funcs, CamelCase types)
Module structure conventions (lib.rs exports, tests/ layout)
Git commit message convention documented
RDF specification layout (.specify/specs/NNN-feature/)
Performance SLO defaults documented
Code style enforced via rustfmt + clippy
VSCode settings auto-applied on repo open (settings.json committed)
Dockerfile/devcontainer for reproducible dev environment

implemented: RUST_LOG, RNG_SEED, RUST_BACKTRACE, CARGO_TERM_COLOR set in .claude/settings.json
Chicago TDD established as mandatory testing pattern
Type-first design documented as elite mindset
RDF/SPARQL as source of truth convention established
gaps: No .editorconfig for cross-editor consistency
No .env.example documenting all env vars
No Dockerfile/devcontainer for reproducible environment
.vscode/settings.json not committed (each user sets up manually)
No git commit message template (.gitmessage)
No conventional commits enforcement (commitlint)
Module structure conventions not enforced
No architecture decision records (ADRs) template
No changelog convention documented
No semantic versioning automation

commitMessageTemplate.contents:
feat|fix|docs|style|refactor|test|chore(scope): description

[Motivation and context]

[Testing performed]

[Receipt]
git log --oneline -1 && cargo make pre-commit

errorHandling.pattern: Result<T, E> always, never unwrap()/expect() in production

testing.pattern: Chicago TDD (real collaborators, AAA pattern, state-based)

examples.without: Must set RUST_LOG, RNG_SEED, configure editor, learn conventions
examples.with: Runs `just test-unit` and RUST_LOG already set to debug in .claude/settings.json

examples.without: Decides: snake_case or camelCase? Result or panic? How to structure module?
examples.with: rustfmt enforces snake_case, clippy enforces Result, .claude/rules/architecture.md shows module structure

examples.without: Decides: mock or real? How to assert? Where to put test file?
examples.with: .claude/rules/rust/testing.md shows Chicago TDD pattern, tests/ layout established, real collaborators expected

examples.without: Decides: where to put file? How to name it? What structure?

criticalInterdependencies.commands → errors: Every command that fails must emit clear, actionable error (dim1 + dim2)
criticalInterdependencies.errors → documentation: Every error code must link to docs (dim2 + dim5)
criticalInterdependencies.workflows → aliases: Every workflow must have aliases to reduce typing (dim3 + dim1)
criticalInterdependencies.ide → documentation: Hover must show docstrings for every symbol (dim4 + dim5)
criticalInterdependencies.iteration → commands: Watch must re-run quick commands (dim6 + dim1)
criticalInterdependencies.defaults → testing: Chicago TDD pattern must be default, not optional (dim7 + dim6)

checks: [ ] All critical recipes in justfile with clear comments
[ ] First run provides helpful guidance if prerequisites missing
[ ] All errors use thiserror for structured types
[ ] Andon signals clearly defined with action items
[ ] All public APIs have docstring with /// comments
[ ] Code style enforced via rustfmt + clippy

items: dim2: Error catalogue docs (GGEN-* codes defined)
dim6: just watch recipe (cargo watch integration)
dim4: Devcontainer / Dockerfile for reproducible env
dim6: Incremental test runner (only changed tests)
